accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text and verified context (DOE program, IIJA statute, FY2027–2031 reauthorization).

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the DOE administration and program identity are accurate context.

states_purpose.why: The summary clearly states the bill reauthorizes and revises DOE's Energy Sector Operational Support for Cyberresilience Program.

changes_to_existing_law.why: It correctly identifies the amendment to Section 40125(c) of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act and the changed authorization period.

exceptions_conditions.why: It notes the sole/unreviewable discretion, no-right-or-benefit clause, FACA nonapplicability, and FOIA disclosure exemption.

effective_dates.why: It correctly captures the authorization period change from FY2022–2026 to FY2027–2031.

major_provisions_covered.why: It captures the new purpose, Energy Threat Analysis Center authority, disclosure exemption, no-right clause, and transaction authority.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive without editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary is well-organized and internally consistent.

concise.why: The summary is appropriately compact without padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: DOE, Secretary of Energy, the IIJA, FOIA, FACA, and the Energy Threat Analysis Center are all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: The only figures stated—the FY2022–2026 to FY2027–2031 change—match the bill text.
